identity.description: Demo Romans 1-11 drift assessment for a South African bank under AI adoption pressure.

decision.summary: Moderate drift risk: high metrics-confidence, some badge reliance, early signs of suffering-avoidance and law-dependence.
decision.notes: Profile is illustrative only; not a real institution.

module1_exchange.notes: Strong pull toward capital, growth, and market share as practical 'ultimate' goods.

module2_hypocrisy.notes: Public ethics messaging outpaces internal audit and reform.

module3_courtroom.notes: Risk and compliance frameworks function mainly as institutional self-defence.

module4_faith_badge.notes: High trust in size, capital, and tech adoption as proof of 'doing the right thing'.

module5_suffering_refinement.notes: Crises mainly met with PR and restructuring, not truth-telling and deep reform.

module6_flesh_spirit.notes: System runs on risk, fear of loss, and performance, not grace or inner renewal.

module7_nation_pruning.notes: Some awareness of past scandals, but mixed follow-through; moderate openness to outside critique.
